message.content:
- The agreement is between an employer and employee.
- The employee agrees to assign all inventions and proprietary rights to the company without additional compensation.
- The employee waives moral rights to all company inventions.
- The employee must disclose all company inventions and maintain records of said inventions.
- The employee will assist the company in obtaining and enforcing proprietary rights.
- The employee must hold all proprietary information in strict confidence.
- The employee cannot improperly use any confidential information from prior employers or disclose it to others.
- The agreement will survive even after termination of employment.
- The company has the right to enforce the agreement through legal and equitable remedies.
- The entire agreement supersedes all prior agreements and discussions.
